relatedness_reasoning: Both files share account_id and customer_id fields; the inbound file provides account balance data that credit department would use to detect arrears, and the outbound file reports arrears flags to risk/compliance—a logical pipeline step within credit risk processing.

mappings.reasoning: Identical field name and purpose; unique account identifier.
Identical field name and purpose; unique customer identifier.
Inbound schema contains no customer name field; likely sourced from a customer master system not present in this inbound file.
Inbound account_type (checking/savings/credit_card) differs from outbound product_type (Mortgage/Personal Loan/Credit Card); partial semantic overlap but enums do not align well.
No arrears duration or status field exists in inbound; this is derived from credit system processing logic not present in account balance data.
No arrears tracking field in inbound; computed by credit system based on payment history not provided in account_balance.json.
Inbound balance field represents current account balance; for arrears accounts this could represent outstanding amount owed, but semantics differ slightly.
No minimum payment field in inbound; this is a credit product attribute not present in account balance snapshot.
No payment due date in inbound; this is a credit product term not captured in account balance data.
No payment history field in inbound; credit system maintains this separately from account balance snapshots.
Risk severity is assigned by credit system logic based on arrears analysis, not present in inbound account balance data.
Risk categorization is determined by credit system processing, not derivable from account balance snapshot alone.
Compliance action determination is a credit system business rule, not present in inbound account balance data.
Action type is assigned by credit system policy, not derivable from account balance information.
Inbound last_updated timestamp could approximate flag creation time, but flag_date is typically when arrears flag is created, not when balance was updated.
Free-text commentary is generated by credit system analysts, not present in structured account balance data.
Inbound sent_at is message timestamp; report_date is when arrears report was generated, likely derived from processing time rather than message send time.
Count of flagged accounts is computed from the arrears_flags array by the credit system, not present in inbound.
Inbound sender is 'accounts_department'; outbound source_department is 'Credit', indicating the report originates from credit department, not accounts.
Destination is hardcoded as 'Risk_and_Compliance' in the outbound schema, not derived from inbound data.
Schema version is a static metadata field, not sourced from inbound account balance data.
